task.title: Test a rule in the interactive REPL
task.shortdesc: Test a rule in the REPL by running the XPath query against a loaded document and then evaluating the full rule object. This confirms your rule will behave as expected before you commit it.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Use the markdown-validator REPL to test XPath expressions and evaluate rule JSON against a loaded document before adding the rule to a rule set.

prereq.p: The REPL is running and a document is loaded. See Start the interactive REPL.

step.cmd: Test your XPath expression with the query command.

info.codeblock:
> query /html/body/h1 text Result: ['My Tutorial Title']
> query //h2 count Result: [3]
> query //h2 text Result: ['Prerequisites', 'Deploy the app', 'Clean up resources']
info.p: Supported flags for query: text, count, dom.

step.stepresult: Confirm the result matches what you expect. If it does not, adjust the XPath and query again.

step.cmd: Evaluate the full rule JSON object with the eval command.

info.p: Pass the rule as a single-line JSON object:

step.stepresult: The REPL prints PASS or FAIL with the rule name: PASS: rule id=5 name='must-have-prerequisites' or: FAIL: rule id=5 name='must-have-prerequisites' → Add a Prerequisites section.

step.cmd: Load a document that should fail the rule and confirm it does.
step.stepresult: The REPL shows FAIL with your mitigation message, confirming the rule catches the intended problem.
